block_01: The end of 2018 brought losses to much of the investment world, oil was not spared.
block_03: Oil (both Brent & WTI) took a tumble of over 40% between October 2018 & December 2018. There were many factors for the fall in oil prices, including over supply, & the U.S. / China trade war; resulting in China (a major importer of U.S. oil) halting imports of up to 500,000 BPD (barrels per day)
block_04: However, it’s 2019, & oil appears to be on the up; monthly candlesticks have been boasting “green” all the way, since January this year. In fact, we are now seeing the longest weekly winning streak since 2016. Since the beginning of the year OIL prices are up 40%.
block_05: There are many factors which can be looked to for the rise; including OPEC & partners, including Russia, adhering to planned output reductions, as well as production losses in members such Venezuela, Iran & Libya, who are each undergoing individual political struggles.
block_06: If you would like to know more about trading commodities with us, including oil, speak to your Account Manager & he or she will be happy to guide you through the latest news & changes, as well as how to apply technical analysis in order to optimise your trading decisions.
